data.title: The WorldCoin team transferred 89.65 million WLD tokens to a new address and then transferred them to exchanges.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, the WorldCoin (@worldnetwork) team has transferred 89.65 million WLD tokens to a new address, worth approximately $26.17 million, and that address has since begun transferring funds to centralized exchanges.

data.title: Samsung Urged to Consider U.S. Listing to Boost Valuation
data.description: A fund manager advocating for Samsung has proposed that the South Korean tech giant list its shares in the United States to enhance its market valuation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential benefits of such a move for the chipmaker. The fund manager believes that a U.S. listing could significantly increase Samsung's visibility and attract more investors, thereby boosting its overall valuation. Samsung, known for its leadership in the semiconductor industry, has been a major player in global markets, and this strategic shift could further solidify its position. The proposal comes amid growing interest in expanding international presence and tapping into the robust U.S. financial markets.

data.title: The US Solomon Islands (SOL) spot ETF saw a total net outflow of $1.041 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (March 26th, Eastern Time), the total net outflow of SOL spot ETFs was $1.041 million. The SOL spot ETF with the largest net outflow yesterday was the Fidelity Solana Fund ETF (FSOL), with a net outflow of $758,200, bringing its historical total net inflow to $150 million. The second largest was the VanEck Solana ETF (VSOL), with a net outflow of $282,800, bringing its historical total net inflow to $18.5371 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of SOL spot ETFs was $850 million, with an SOL net asset ratio of 1.72%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $993 million.

data.title: Revolut Tops List of European Neobanks in March 2026
data.description: Revolut has been ranked as the leading European neobank based on user scale, crypto product depth, geographic reach, and integration with everyday finance as of March 2026. According to NS3.AI, the list positions N26 in second place, followed by bunq, Monzo, and Lydia. These rankings reflect the competitive landscape of digital banking in Europe, highlighting the strengths and market presence of each neobank.

data.title: UK Prime Minister Stands Firm Amid Tensions with U.S. President Trump
data.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has indicated he will not be intimidated by U.S. President Donald Trump, highlighting the strain in the historically close ties between the United Kingdom and the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing the impact of Trump's aggressive stance on Iran, which has contributed to the tension between the two nations. The situation underscores the challenges in maintaining the 'special relationship' amidst differing foreign policy approaches.

data.title: Bitcoin Recovery Timeline Estimated at 300 Days After 45% Drawdown
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Ecoinometrics has projected that Bitcoin may require approximately 300 days to regain its previous all-time high following a significant 45% decline. The platform emphasized that this estimate serves as a guideline for the recovery timeframe rather than a direct price prediction.

data.title: China Expresses Concerns Over U.S. Trade Investigations
data.description: Chinese Commerce Minister Wang Wentao has expressed significant concerns to U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer regarding new trade investigations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that both nations are aiming to stabilize relations ahead of a potential presidential summit. The discussions underscore ongoing tensions between the two economic powers, as they navigate complex trade dynamics.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump: White House Shares Cryptic Pixelated Images
data.description: On March 27, Jin10 reported that the U.S. White House has released three pixelated images resembling U.S. President Donald Trump on social media. According to Jin10, this follows the previous day's posting of two ambiguous videos. The recent posts were shared without any accompanying text, leaving the public speculating about their meaning and intent.

data.title: Britons Increase Savings Amid Economic Uncertainty from Iran Conflict
data.description: Britons are increasingly saving money and reducing spending on major purchases as they navigate the economic repercussions of the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting a survey that reveals this trend among consumers. The survey indicates a shift in consumer behavior as individuals prioritize financial security in response to the uncertain economic environment. This change reflects broader concerns about the potential impact of geopolitical tensions on the economy. As the situation in Iran continues to unfold, consumers are taking precautionary measures to safeguard their financial well-being.

data.title: California Gas Prices Surge Amid Cost-of-Living Crisis
data.description: Gas prices in California have surged to nearly $6 per gallon, exacerbating the ongoing cost-of-living crisis in the state. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the financial strain this places on residents already grappling with high housing and living expenses. The increase in fuel costs is attributed to a combination of factors, including supply chain disruptions and rising crude oil prices. This development adds pressure on consumers and businesses alike, as transportation costs rise and disposable incomes shrink. The situation underscores the broader economic challenges facing Californians, as they navigate the impacts of inflation and economic uncertainty.

data.title: GameStop did not sell any Bitcoin; instead, it pledged 4,709 BTC for an options strategy.
data.description: GameStop disclosed that it did not sell Bitcoin, but instead pledged approximately 4,709 BTC to Coinbase Credit for a covered call option strategy. Previously, the market had speculated that it intended to liquidate its Bitcoin holdings after transferring all its BTC to Coinbase Prime. The filing shows that the company generated revenue by selling short-term options with strike prices between $105,000 and $110,000, and currently maintains relevant Bitcoin economic exposure, holding only 1 BTC directly. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: FCC Chairman Proposes Collective Negotiation for TV Networks with Sports Leagues
data.description: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr has proposed that broadcast television networks be permitted to negotiate collectively with sports leagues. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Carr's suggestion as a strategy to reduce programming expenses. This proposal aims to address the rising costs associated with sports broadcasting rights, which have been a significant financial burden for networks. By allowing collective bargaining, networks could potentially leverage better deals, ultimately benefiting consumers with lower costs.

data.title: Expert Warns of Bitcoin's Rising Wedge as Sell Signal
data.description: Peter Brandt has identified a rising wedge pattern in Bitcoin's current market behavior, describing it as a potential sell signal. According to NS3.AI, Brandt highlighted $65,000 as a critical support level for Bitcoin. The rising wedge is a classic technical pattern often indicating a reversal during an uptrend, suggesting caution for investors.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Considers Deploying Additional Troops to Middle East, Wall Street Journal Reports
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly contemplating the deployment of an additional 10,000 ground troops to the Middle East, according to the Wall Street Journal. This move comes amid heightened tensions in the region, with the U.S. aiming to bolster its military presence to address ongoing security concerns.

According to Jin10, the decision is part of a broader strategy to enhance the United States' defensive capabilities in response to perceived threats. The potential deployment underscores the administration's commitment to safeguarding American interests and allies in the Middle East.

The proposal is currently under review, with discussions ongoing among senior military officials and advisors. The final decision will likely consider various factors, including regional stability and the potential impact on diplomatic relations.

This development follows recent escalations in the area, prompting the U.S. to reassess its military strategy. The administration's focus remains on ensuring the safety and security of American personnel and assets stationed abroad.

Further details on the deployment plan are expected to emerge as deliberations continue, with the administration weighing the strategic benefits against potential risks.

data.title: China's Economic Shift Toward Reflation Could Boost Corporate Earnings and Stock Gains
data.description: China's anticipated move towards reflation appears imminent, potentially revitalizing corporate earnings and driving stock market gains. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the optimism surrounding this economic transition. Analysts suggest that this shift could enhance investor confidence and stimulate economic growth, as businesses may benefit from improved earnings prospects. The move is expected to have significant implications for the financial markets, with potential ripple effects across global economies. Observers are closely monitoring China's policy adjustments, which could mark a pivotal moment in its economic strategy.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Considers October IPO Amidst Competition with OpenAI
data.description: Anthropic, an artificial intelligence company, is reportedly contemplating an initial public offering (IPO) as early as October. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's efforts to compete with rival OpenAI in the race to go public. This move comes as both companies seek to capitalize on the growing interest and investment in AI technologies. The potential IPO would mark a significant milestone for Anthropic, positioning it alongside other major players in the AI industry. Details about the IPO, including the valuation and specific timeline, remain undisclosed.

data.title: UK Car Production Declines by 17.2% in February, SMMT Reports
data.description: The Society of Motor Manufacturers and Traders (SMMT) has reported a significant decrease in the UK's car production for February, with total output falling by 17.2% to 68,061 units. According to Jin10, this decline reflects ongoing challenges in the automotive sector, including supply chain disruptions and economic uncertainties. The reduction in production highlights the pressures faced by manufacturers as they navigate a complex market environment. The SMMT's report underscores the need for strategic adjustments to address these challenges and support the industry's recovery.

data.title: Bond Traders Seek Hedging Amid Rising Iran Conflict Concerns
data.description:
Bond traders are increasingly anxious about the potential escalation of the Iran conflict, prompting them to seek hedges against the worst-case scenario of a forced Federal Reserve rate hike in the coming weeks. According to PANews, there is a growing demand in the options market for bets linked to the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR), anticipating a rate hike as soon as two weeks from now.

These trades could benefit if the bond market significantly raises its rate hike expectations before the Federal Reserve's policy meeting on April 29. The surge in demand for hedges against an emergency rate hike marks a sharp reversal in market sentiment.

Jeff Schuh, head of the rates department at Constitution Capital, noted that while the latest bets do not reflect the market's baseline scenario, they do indicate increasing concerns that rapidly rising inflation could pose risks to investors who have been long on U.S. Treasuries in recent months.

Currently, the interest rate swap market only prices in a three-basis-point increase for the April 29 policy meeting, equating to a 12% probability of a 25-basis-point hike.

data.title: Iranian Media Reports Thai-Flagged Cargo Ship Stranded Near Iran's Qeshm Island After Hormuz Strait Attack
data.description: A cargo ship flying the Thai flag has reportedly been attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, leading to its stranding near Iran's Qeshm Island, according to Tasnim News Agency. The incident highlights ongoing tensions in the strategically vital waterway, which is a crucial passage for global oil shipments. Details surrounding the attack and the condition of the vessel remain unclear, as authorities investigate the circumstances surrounding the event.

data.title: Google Unveils Technology for More Efficient AI Storage
data.description: Google has announced a new technology aimed at enhancing the efficiency of AI storage. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential impact of this development on the tech industry. Meanwhile, Cathay Pacific is set to increase its fuel surcharge by 34% starting next month, reflecting changes in the aviation sector. Additionally, U.S. President Donald Trump has once again delayed actions against Iran's energy facilities, indicating ongoing geopolitical tensions.
